subject: Public Health
title: Confounding: The Hidden Variable
description: A 60-second lesson on Confounding: The Hidden Variable.

hook.text: Coffee drinkers have higher lung cancer rates—but it's not the coffee. Smokers drink more coffee. That's confounding.

buildup.text: A confounder is a third variable associated with both the exposure and the outcome—distorting the true relationship.

discovery.text: Confounding can make a harmless exposure look dangerous—or hide a real effect entirely.

twist.text: Randomization in trials eliminates confounding. In observational studies, statistical methods like stratification.

climax.text: Recognizing and controlling for confounders is one of the most critical skills in epidemiology.

punchline.text: If you don't look for confounders, they'll trick you every time.

quiz.question: What is a confounder?
options: A variable associated with both exposure and outcome
A measurement error in the study
